title: Podman play kube container's PATH does not use variables set in Dockerfile / docker build
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

While using an image which has something like `ENV PATH="$PATH:/bin/aux"` in its Dockerfile, while running as a container the path appears to be set from whatever the host's machine's PATH env variable is at that time. This presents issues when critical binaries are located in /bin/aux. A workaround is to set the PATH to include /bin/aux in the pod yaml file, however this is not required in other systems like docker compose.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create a pod.yaml file which has an image that alters the PATH environment variable, ie. k3s is a good example
2. Run podman play kube pod.yaml

**Describe the results you received:**

The container's PATH does not include what is defined in the Dockerfile.

**Describe the results you expected:**

The container's PATH does include what is defined in the Dockerfile.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

N/A

**Output of `podman version`:**

Version:      3.0.1
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.2
Built:        Wed Dec 31 18:00:00 1969
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +CRIU +YAJL
  os: linux

  slirp4netns:

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I can take a look at this one if it is still open.

Thanks @EduardoVega. I haven't had a lot of time to walk through this issue, however I did find that docker uses this function to build out the environment vars ReplaceOrAppendEnvValues on the container:

https://github.com/moby/moby/blob/8eb947c5b1d7ce7551ed6e07db7a8b65234b75e0/container/env.go#L9

Hopefully this helps!

@kellen-dunham I haven't been able to replicate your issue, so please let me know if I'm missing something here.

Currently I am using fedora with podman version:
```
Version:      3.1.2
API Version:  3.1.2
Go Version:   go1.14
Git Commit:   51b8ddbc22cf5b10dd76dd9243924aa66ad7db39
Built:        Thu Apr 22 11:23:22 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```


1. Containerfile / Dockerfile
  ```
  FROM fedora
 
  ENV PATH="$PATH:/bin/aux"

  CMD tail -f /dev/null
  ```
2. Build image
  ```
  podman build --tag 9700 .
  ```
3. Run container and check `$PATH` env var
  ```
  $ podman run --rm 9700 sh -c 'echo $PATH'
  /usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin:/bin/aux
  ```
4. pod.yaml
```
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  labels:
    app: watermelon
  name: watermelon
spec:
  containers:
  - command:
    - sh
    args:
    - -c
    - sleep 3600
    image: 9700
    name: ctr01
    securityContext:
      allowPrivilegeEscalation: true
      capabilities:
        drop:
        - CAP_MKNOD
        - CAP_NET_RAW
        - CAP_AUDIT_WRITE
      privileged: false
      readOnlyRootFilesystem: false
```
5. Create pod using play kube and check `$PATH` env var
  ```
  $ podman play kube pod.yaml
  $ podman exec watermelon-ctr01 sh -c 'echo $PATH'
  /usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin:/bin/aux
  ```
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Since we never heard response back from @kellen-dunham I am going to assume this problem went away or is fixed in newer versions of Podman.  Please reopen if you have further information and get it to fail on the current code in the main branch.
